response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack engineer with solid software craftsmanship fundamentals, but his profile is deeply misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project — far from the 8–12 years of hands-on C++/STL development the position requires. His career has been consistently oriented toward web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, DLang), with no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, industrial communication protocols (MQTT at scale, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443 security standards, or industrial automation domain knowledge.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code quality practices evident throughout career
Event-driven and distributed-systems thinking demonstrated via GISCollective platform and high-throughput micro-services work at Dunnhumby
Polyglot adaptability and willingness to mentor, run workshops, and share knowledge — relevant to the people-leadership dimension of the role
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated: ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the depth required for architectural-level C++/STL work
Zero evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, industrial communication protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0), or IEC 62443 / SDL security practices
Entire post-2015 career is in web/TypeScript/Node.js; no multi-threaded systems, MQTT, or embedded/on-premises platform work
